deductions.description: Bent arms during block phase on table (KTM)
Lack of full hip extension/low hips at block, reducing vertical drive
Slight pike in post-flight, not a clean flat body line
Leg separation and slight knee bend in post-flight (KTM)
Deep squat on landing to absorb impact
Small hop forward and arm circle for balance on landing

deduction_log.reason: The Handspring Vault demonstrated good power and height, but execution suffered from several form breaks during the block and post-flight, as well as a deep squat and hop on landing.

special_requirements.comment: A Handspring Vault is an A-level vault and fulfills the requirement.

artistry.notes: General body tension and tightness could be improved throughout the vault, particularly in the post-flight and landing. While vault has less artistry than other events, clean lines and controlled movement contribute to overall presentation.

coaching_summary: The gymnast performed a Handspring Vault with commendable power and height, which is a strong foundation for Xcel Gold. However, the routine was marred by several technical imperfections. The block phase showed bent arms and insufficient hip drive, leading to a less-than-ideal body shape in the post-flight (slight pike, leg separation, and knee bend). The landing required a deep squat and a hop with arm adjustments, indicating a lack of control and stacked CoM. Addressing these foundational issues will significantly improve execution and readiness for higher levels.
top_3_fixes: Strengthen the block by focusing on locked arms and active shoulder push, driving hips vertically for a tight hollow shape off the table.
Maintain a rigid, hollow body position throughout the post-flight to eliminate pike, knee bend, and leg separation.
Improve landing control by practicing drills that emphasize a stacked center of mass over the base of support, aiming for a soft, controlled landing with minimal squat and no steps/hops.
celebrations: Excellent power and height achieved in the vault, demonstrating good athleticism.
